items.title: Australia announces squad for Women's T20 Cricket World Cup, Darcie Brown left out
items.summary: Brisbane Heat left-arm pace bowler Lucy Hamilton has been selected in Australia's T20 World Cup squad with the experienced Darcie Brown a surprise omission.

items.title: Goldfields women's shelter forced to adapt as abusers use tracking technology
items.summary: A women's shelter in regional Western Australia has had to adapt to an increase in technology-facilitated violence to keep victims safe.

items.title: Political fight brewing over budget tax reform as Coalition vows to fight Labor's changes
items.summary: The Coalition vows to do all it can to prevent property tax reform, which the shadow treasurer says will increase rents and make it harder for young people to buy a house.

items.title: Southampton beats Middlesbrough 2-1 in extra time to reach EFL Championship playoff final
items.summary: Southampton has defeated Middlesbrough to advance to the EFL Championship play-off final after a tense and heated two-leg semifinal.

items.summary: How much the budget measures could benefit you depends on how much you earn.

items.title: Wil Anderson hates advertising. So why does he host a show about it?

items.title: MV Hondius passengers quarantine, fight disease as Dutch and UK governments order isolations
items.summary: The UK has ordered 10 people from remote island territories go into isolation, while parachuting in medical supplies, clinicians over suspected hantavirus case.

items.title: Sunrisers Hyderabad bowled out for 86 in IPL loss to Gujarat Titans
items.summary: Australian Test cricketers Travis Head and Pat Cummins have been part of a heavy loss in the Indian Premier League.

items.title: Iran war boosts Beijing ahead of Trump-Xi summit in China, experts say
items.summary: As US President Donald Trump prepares to meet his Chinese counterpart in Beijing, some analysts say the war in Iran has significantly shifted the power dynamics in China's favour.

items.title: Georgia rugby players, including former captain, banned over drug test scheme
items.summary: Former Georgia captain Merab Sharikadze was one of six players handed lengthy bans from rugby for their part in a scheme involving alleged sample substitution and advance warnings of drug tests.

items.title: Former Carlton AFL coach Michael Voss says club in better place since he took over

items.title: Commonwealth Bank shares slump 10.4pc in biggest one-day fall on record
items.summary: It was the worst day on the stock market for Australia's biggest bank since March 2020, when it dropped about 10 per cent, with sentiment also weighed by a trading update and broader weakness in bank stocks.

items.title: PM defends tax overhaul as Coalition accuses him of 'lying' — as it happened
items.summary: The Coalition accuses the prime minister of "lying" about changes to tax settings as Anthony Albanese defends the overhaul as necessary for younger Australians.

items.title: Jannik Sinner equals record with Novak Djokovic for consecutive Masters 1000 match wins
items.summary: World number one Jannik Sinner extended his Masters 1000 winning streak to 31 matches, equalling Novak Djokovic's all-time record.

items.title: Federal budget disappointment for farmers and environmental groups
items.summary: Weed and pest control programs have been cut in the federal budget as farmers and conservationists grapple with feral animals and noxious plants.

items.title: Midnight Oil frontman Peter Garrett on 'closing the circle' to chair Landcare Australia
items.summary: The former frontman for the Australian rockers was among those who helped lobby for the formation of Landcare Australia in 1989, so it seems fitting he should circle back to chair the organisation after a lifetime lobbying for the environment.

items.title: Trump labels critical news reports on Iran 'virtual treason' - as it happened
items.summary: US President Donald Trump says he does not need help from Beijing to end the war with Iran but says he will talk at length with Chinese President Xi Jinping when they hold a summit this week.

items.title: High Court hears bid to overturn block on 22-year Mount Pleasant coal mine extension
items.summary: The High Court will hear a major climate change case that could affect mining approvals, as the operator of the Mount Pleasant coal mine seeks to overturn a ruling blocking the project's extension.

items.title: Australian-born, London-based MP at centre of 'palace coup' plot to oust Britsh PM Keir Starmer
items.summary: Up until the past few days, Catherine West kept a low profile, as far as politicians go. Most Brits would never have come across the Australian-born, London-based MP before, but that all changed at the weekend.

items.title: Victorian emergency department nurse faces deportation due to son's condition

items.title: Native Pacific black ducks at risk in Tasmania due to introduced species

items.title: Australia-UAE defence commitments do not involve offensive action against Iran, says government
items.summary: The Albanese government insists Australia is not involved in any "offensive action against Iran" amid reports the UAE, which had been supplied a Wedgetail aircraft, carried out military strikes against the country.

items.title: Will CGT and negative gearing budget changes make housing cheaper for first home buyers?
items.summary: There's no doubt that taking away negative gearing on existing properties will reduce the amount many investors can afford to pay, evening up the playing field for owner-occupiers. But these tax changes by themselves aren't a cure all.

items.title: Jayson Gillham on legal battle with Melbourne Symphony Orchestra and his tour Keys to Life
items.summary: In 2024, Jayson Gillham's show was cancelled for his on-stage comments about Palestine. Now he brings a legal suit and a new recital tour with a Palestinian Jordanian artist.

items.title: Labor bets the house on young Australians' desperation to buy their own
items.summary: If the reforms help younger Australians believe home ownership is within reach, Labor can expect to collect a political dividend.

items.summary: A Perth father was allegedly high on 'nangs' during a crash that killed his two daughters, as a scuffle with media erupts at court.

items.title: Duo swindled $6 million from five victims in fraudulent property investment scam, court hears
items.summary: Two men fleeced millions from five victims in a fraudulent property investment scheme, which one victim said had stolen half her superannuation and "ruined" her life.

items.title: Izak Rankine says he has bounced back from suspension for homophobic slur

items.title: Queensland government says it will continue with Paradise Dam rebuild despite advice to ditch project
items.summary: The board of Sunwater has written to the state government, labelling the rebuild of Paradise Dam "grossly disproportionate" and should be "no further considered".

items.summary: Owned by the Holmes à Court family, Heytesbury runs 165,000 head of cattle in northern Australia and has just struck a deal with Vroon to buy its ships.

items.title: Murdoch University promises improvements after viral video shows issues with wheelchair access

items.title: Year 7 boy trapped under train at North Melbourne station had bag snagged by mirror, school says
items.summary: The St Aloysius College student remains in a serious condition in hospital after the incident yesterday afternoon.

items.title: Multiple sclerosis patient spends night at Adelaide hospital waiting for an access taxi

items.title: Four ministers quit as UK PM Starmer tells cabinet he is staying put
items.summary: The British prime minister has defied calls to resign, telling ministers he will "get on with governing".

items.title: Racist attacks leave New Zealand's Indian community feeling 'hounded'

items.summary: Carlton CEO Graham Wright says the club will embark on a thorough process to determine the successor to Michael Voss.

items.summary: What started out as abstinence during a pregnancy four years ago turned into a game with myself to see how long I could go without alcohol.

items.summary: As a peace deal between the US and Iran remains elusive, low-wage seafarers are in limbo as they witness an unprecedented 21st-century battle.

items.summary: Michael Voss has left Carlton, resigning with immediate effect following a run of seven consecutive AFL defeats, with another former Carlton boss Mick Malthouse saying the Brisbane premiership-winning player will be relieved to be out of the hot seat.

items.title: Bullsbrook Centre for National Resilience to be used to quarantine passengers from hantavirus ship
items.summary: Nestled away in Perth's outer fringes this 500-bed facility was built during the COVID-19 pandemic as a quarantine centre, but sat idle for years. Now, a different deadly virus will see it used for its intended purpose.

items.summary: Six ministerial aides have stepped down and more than 70 UK Labour MPs have publicly called for Sir Keir Starmer's resignation.

items.title: Volunteer gives central Queensland hospice residents free haircuts

items.title: AFL needs to do more to encourage Indigenous players as marked decline continues at top level
items.summary: The AFL is considering a range of measures to boost the number of Indigenous players in the league following a significant decline in recent seasons.

items.title: Health minister invited to spend week with regional NDIS family
items.summary: Regional families are anxiously waiting to see the impact of recently announced changes to the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS), especially for those with autism.
